_comment: Scenario 18: Perception-Driven Obstacle Avoidance — no HD-map, D* Lite dynamic layer only

scenario.name: perception_avoidance
scenario.description: Navigate through an obstacle field using camera+radar perception — no static HD-map. The color_contour detector finds obstacles, ByteTrack maintains tracks, UKF fuses camera depth + radar range/velocity, and D* Lite replans dynamically via update_from_objects(). Validates the full multi-sensor perception→planning loop for unknown environments. ObstacleAvoider3D is minimized so D* Lite is the primary avoidance mechanism.

obstacle_avoidance._comment: Lateral-only reactive avoidance — vertical_gain=0 prevents altitude runaway from objects below the drone. D* Lite dynamic layer is primary.

occupancy_grid._comment: 3s TTL keeps detected obstacles in the grid long enough for replanning

mission_planner._comment_static_obstacles: Empty — no HD-map. All obstacle knowledge comes from camera detections at runtime.

geofence._comment: Geofence disabled — D* Lite replanning may push drone toward boundary when avoiding detected obstacles

waypoints._comment: Fly north — RED Gz(7,1)=our(1,7) visible to east. Drone should detect and mark in grid.
Fly east — path passes 3m from RED. D* Lite should route around if grid cell is occupied.
Continue east — GREEN Gz(8,8)=our(8,8) and YELLOW Gz(15,7)=our(7,15) visible ahead/side.
Fly south — clear corridor east of obstacle field.
Return home — ORANGE Gz(4,4)=our(4,4) near path, drone should route around.

mission_planner._comment_waypoints: Waypoints route AROUND the obstacle field, not through it. Obstacles are visible from the flight path so the camera can detect them and populate the D* Lite grid. This validates detection+grid population without requiring instant reaction time.

tracker._comment: ByteTrack two-stage association for robust obstacle tracking

radar._comment: Radar enabled — camera+radar fusion validates full multi-sensor pipeline
Camera+radar UKF fusion with ground-plane filter — rejects radar returns below 0.3m AGL
